2604.03071.title: Automatic Textbook Formalization
2604.03071.paper_summary: We present a case study where an automatic AI system formalizes a textbook with more than 500 pages of graduate-level algebraic combinatorics to Lean. The resulting formalization represents a new milestone in textbook formalization scale and proficiency, moving from early results in undergraduate topology and restructuring of existing library content to a full standalone formalization of a graduate textbook. The formalization comprises 130K lines of code and 5900 Lean declarations and was conducted within one week by a total of 30K Claude 4.5 Opus agents collaborating in parallel on a shared code base via version control, simultaneously setting a record in multi-agent software engineering with usable results. The inference cost matches or undercuts what we estimate as the salaries required for a team of human experts, and we expect there is still the potential for large efficiencies to be made without the need for better models. We make our code, the resulting Lean code base and a side-by-side blueprint website available open-source.
2604.03071.paper_summary_zh: 我們展示了一個案例研究，其中一個自動化的 AI 系統將一本超過 500 頁的研究生級別代數組合數學教科書形式化為 Lean。這一成果的形式化代表了教科書形式化規模和熟練度的新里程碑，從早期的本科拓撲結果和現有庫內容的重組，轉變為一部研究生教科書的完整獨立形式化。這一形式化包含 130K 行代碼和 5900 個 Lean 聲明，並在一週內由總共 30K 的 Claude 4.5 Opus 代理協作完成，通過版本控制在共享代碼庫上並行工作，同時在多代理軟體工程中創下了可用結果的紀錄。推理成本與我們估算的人類專家團隊所需的薪資相匹配或低於預期，我們預計在不需要更好模型的情況下，仍然有潛力實現大規模的效率提升。我們將我們的代碼、生成的 Lean 代碼庫以及一個並排的藍圖網站以開源方式提供。
2604.03071.author: Fabian Gloeckle et.al.
2604.03071.authors: Fabian Gloeckle, Ahmad Rammal, Charles Arnal, Remi Munos, Vivien Cabannes, Gabriel Synnaeve, Amaury Hayat
